title: Kaju Katli

subs.name: pistachio or almond, or a 50:50 mix of both, for a different flavour profile and colour
caster sugar, which dissolves faster and reduces the risk of crystallisation
coconut oil for greasing — the katli will still set cleanly

steps.text: Make sure the cashews are completely dry — even slight moisture will make them turn into paste instead of powder. Pulse them in a dry mixie jar in 5-second bursts until you get a fine, dry powder. Sieve out any coarser pieces and re-pulse them. The powder should flow freely and not clump.

Combine sugar and water in a heavy-bottomed kadai over medium heat, stirring until the sugar fully dissolves. Stop stirring and cook without touching for 5–6 minutes until you reach a single-thread consistency: dip a clean spoon, let it cool 10 seconds, then press a drop between wet thumb and forefinger — when pulled apart, a single thread should form and hold without breaking.

Remove the kadai from heat and quickly tip in all the cashew powder and cardamom at once. Stir vigorously to combine — the mixture will look crumbly at first. Return to the lowest flame and stir continuously for 3–4 minutes, pressing any lumps against the pan, until the mixture pulls together into a smooth, soft, non-sticky mass that leaves the sides cleanly.

Grease a sheet of parchment paper or thick aluminium foil with a thin layer of ghee. Turn the warm cashew mixture onto it — it will be very hot — and place a second greased sheet on top. Use a rolling pin or the flat bottom of a steel tumbler to press and roll to an even 4–5 mm thickness while the mixture is still warm and pliable. Work swiftly as it stiffens as it cools.

Scatter chopped pistachios over the surface and press lightly. Leave to cool and firm at room temperature for 20–25 minutes. Use a sharp knife or pizza cutter to cut into diamond (rhombus) shapes — make parallel cuts in one direction, then diagonal cuts. Lift each piece carefully with a flat spatula. Store layered between parchment in an airtight box at room temperature for up to a week.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: cashew → pistachio or almond, or a 50:50 mix of both, for a different flavour profile and colour; ghee → coconut oil for greasing — the katli will still set cleanly; sugar → caster sugar, which dissolves faster and reduces the risk of crystallisation
